title: Marketing Research and Public Opinion Polling

narrative.executive_view: Marketing research and public-opinion polling systematically gathers, records, tabulates and presents marketing and opinion data under the official NAICS definition [S1]. AI can quickly reduce the cost of questionnaire drafting, coding, analysis, charting and report production, but this is a pressured rather than clean value-migration case: low-end studies face self-service and synthetic alternatives, while human samples, methodological accountability and trusted client counsel remain valuable. The deterministic occupation proxy is unusable because 4-digit 5419 is dominated by veterinary siblings, so this is a low-confidence investment screen rather than a staffing measurement.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: The immediate AI use cases are research design drafts, open-end coding, transcript summarization, tabulation assistance, analysis narratives and proposal writing. Greenbook's 2025 GRIT report describes AI adoption as a permanent market shift while emphasizing human-centered insights and a trust problem around 'good enough' work [S2]. The ICC/ESOMAR code separately defines a human individual in contrast to a synthetic person [S3], a useful reminder that AI can accelerate the workflow without necessarily substituting for the respondent or the accountable researcher. Senior methodology, recruitment for difficult samples and client decision support remain less replaceable.
narrative.value_capture: Project work is often competitively bid, so savings from programming, coding and reporting are likely to be shared with clients rather than retained in full. Retainers, trackers, proprietary panels and regulated/high-consequence work can retain more value because the buyer is paying for continuity, quality control and defensible human data. I set distribution at 35% and moat at 40%: these are intentionally conservative judgments reflecting price competition and founder/key-researcher dependency, offset partly by trust, methods and client integration. They do not use the human-data floor to inflate capture; that floor is considered under terminal demand.
narrative.adoption_timing: The reopened GRIT page establishes a strong qualitative industry shift, but it does not provide a public NAICS-wide effective-adoption percentage [S2]. I estimate current effective adoption at 45% and 2.5 years to 50%, distinguishing embedded workflow use from occasional generative-AI experimentation. That keeps t50 positive as required by the stated adoption estimate and allows for procurement, privacy, validation and client-trust constraints.
narrative.consolidation_economics: Visible strategic consolidation exists but is tilted toward large data, panels and technology rather than the ordinary $1–10m EBITDA agency: Kantar completed its Numerator acquisition [S4], NIQ agreed to acquire Gastrograph AI [S5] and reports nine tuck-ins [S6], and WPP acquired InfoSum for AI-enabled marketing intelligence [S7]. These are evidence of strategic buyer interest, not comparable private-company terms. I use a 3.5x buy and 4.25x exit against Windsor Drake's adjacent 3.0x–4.5x $1m–$3m and 4.0x–6.5x $3m–$10m marketing/digital-agency ranges [S8]; key-person and substitution risks keep the estimate low-confidence.
narrative.terminal_demand: Paid demand is not simply labor demand. The ESOMAR code defines primary data as information collected directly from or about an individual for research [S3], and its treatment of the human individual separately from a synthetic entity reflects a continuing quality and ethics distinction. Yet low-stakes exploratory work, survey programming and analysis can move to DIY tools or synthetic alternatives. I classify the code as PRESSURED: human panels, careful sampling and defensible interpretation can remain durable, but they are not guaranteed to protect a generic boutique from budget deflation or insourcing.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The code boundary excludes many data/analytics and DIY-platform businesses, while the large acquisitions cited here span technology, panels, consumer intelligence and marketing data rather than a clean US 541910 lower-middle-market sample. The supplied 4-digit 5419 role mix contains veterinary occupations and cannot identify research staffing; the residual role judgment is therefore a transparent modeling device. No reopened source supplied a code-specific owner-age distribution, succession shortage, or disclosed buy/exit multiple. Those values are estimates with explicit caveats, and polling headlines should not be generalized to the larger commercial-research segment.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 541910
sources.what_it_supports: Official definition: establishments systematically gather, record, tabulate and present marketing and public-opinion data.

sources.title: 2025 GRIT Business & Innovation Report
sources.what_it_supports: Greenbook describes AI adoption as a permanent market shift while emphasizing human-centered insights and a trust problem around good-enough insights.

sources.title: ICC/ESOMAR International Code on Market, Opinion and Social Research and Data Analytics, 2025
sources.what_it_supports: The code defines primary data and distinguishes a human individual from a synthetic, virtual or digitally created persona.

sources.title: Kantar acquisition of Numerator completes
sources.what_it_supports: Kantar completed acquisition of Numerator, a tech-driven consumer and market-intelligence company; financial terms were not released.

sources.title: NIQ signs agreement to acquire Gastrograph AI
sources.what_it_supports: NIQ announced acquisition of a sensory-insights AI platform to provide faster, lower-cost product innovation capabilities.

sources.title: NIQ acquires Mtrix
sources.what_it_supports: NIQ states the Mtrix acquisition was its latest of nine tuck-ins enhancing analytics and retailer tools.

sources.title: WPP acquires InfoSum
sources.what_it_supports: WPP acquired InfoSum to provide AI-enabled marketing intelligence and privacy-safe data collaboration.

sources.title: Windsor Drake EBITDA Multiples by Industry, 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Its Q1 2026 observations show marketing/digital-agency EBITDA ranges of 3.0x–4.5x for $1m–$3m and 4.0x–6.5x for $3m–$10m, with retention and specialization as drivers.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541910 ($8,069,771,700) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541910 ($26,698,162,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: Veterinary Assistants and Laboratory Animal Caretakers
Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $318,207 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541910); class <5: 2,509 firms, avg revenue/firm $530,196 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $82,976 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 435 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,835,349 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $287,232 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 296 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,275,365 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $512,595 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 309 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,129,165 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,585,214 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 120 firms, avg revenue/firm $46,203,783 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,230,892 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 78 firms, avg revenue/firm $191,354,487 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $29,946,977 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 429
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: The supplied proxy is dominated by veterinary occupations, so the listed non-research roles are assigned cautiously and the residual is the transparent approximation for actual research, operations and qualitative labor. AI accelerates analysis and reporting, not human sampling or senior counsel.
caveats: 4-digit 5419 is materially contaminated by veterinary services and is not a usable representation of 541910 staffing.
The high residual share is a modeling consequence of the bad proxy, not an observed occupation distribution.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Veterinary-technician proxy is out of scope for marketing research.

breakdown_by_role.role: Veterinary Assistants and Laboratory Animal Caretakers
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Veterinary-assistant proxy is out of scope for marketing research.
Veterinarian proxy is out of scope for marketing research.
Scheduling and routine communication automate, but not all client coordination.
Animal-caretaker proxy is out of scope.
Routine intake, follow-up and administrative service automate substantially.
Resourcing and project reporting automate; escalation and methodology governance remain.
Photography is not a core research proxy; limited automated post-production is allowed.

breakdown_by_role.role: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Proposal drafting automates, while client development remains relationship-led.
Transcription/translation aids automate, but interpretation and cultural judgment remain.

Residual is the bounded stand-in for research analysts, field/panel operations, qualitative staff and back office; AI is strong in analysis and reporting but not in human-data collection or senior counsel.

pi_dist.rationale: Competitive project bids and AI-native alternatives should pass savings through; trackers, proprietary data and higher-stakes work retain a portion.
caveats: Sources support AI/data competitive change, not a measured 541910 pass-through rate.

pi_moat.rationale: Methodological trust, human-data quality and embedded trackers protect some value, while founder relationships and DIY alternatives limit transferability.
caveats: No source in this packet measures lower-middle-market client retention or founder dependency.

t50_years.rationale: The industry shift is visible, but governance, validation and respondent-quality requirements slow embedded adoption beyond casual tool use.
caveats: No public full-industry effective-adoption percentage was reopened.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: 45% expresses substantial but sub-50% effective workflow adoption, inferred from GRIT's qualitative AI-shift evidence rather than claimed as a survey result.
caveats: Not an observed NAICS adoption statistic; it intentionally remains below 50%, requiring positive t50.

historical_analogs.value: DIY survey tools and analytics platforms shifted simple data collection and reporting toward self-service; AI extends that pressure to drafting, analysis and synthesis while leaving human-data validity differentiated.
historical_analogs.rationale: The sources support AI/data-platform change and the human-versus-synthetic distinction; the analogue is a disclosed judgment.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Low-end substitution with selective persistence of high-trust research
caveats: No causal historical study is claimed.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: No reopened code-specific owner-age survey was found; 30% is a conservative planning assumption for founder-led boutiques.
caveats: Unobserved estimate; not a demographic claim.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No reopened source documents a 541910 succession shortage.
succession_shortage_documented.plausible_range: false unless code-specific evidence is obtained
caveats: Absence of evidence here is not evidence of absence.

active_consolidators.rationale: Kantar, NIQ and WPP each announced or completed a strategic data/insights acquisition.
active_consolidators.plausible_range: At least 3 visible strategic buyers; lower-middle-market buyer count unknown
caveats: These buyers and targets are global/data/technology adjacent and are not direct evidence of small US 541910 acquisition appetite.

buy_mult.rationale: Within the low end of adjacent marketing-agency ranges, reflecting concentrated clients, key-person dependency and substitution risk.
caveats: No disclosed lower-middle-market 541910 transaction multiple was reopened; the cited marketing-agency category is a proxy.

exit_mult.rationale: A modest platform premium is possible for recurring trackers, proprietary panels and retained staff, but remains below a technology-data premium.
caveats: Strategic announcements disclose no transaction multiples and are materially larger/adjacent to the intended target set.

pricing_structure.value: Fixed-price custom studies and competitive RFPs, with stickier tracker/retainer and proprietary-panel revenue where applicable.
pricing_structure.rationale: The official scope and industry discussion support a research-services model; the pricing split is a disclosed market judgment.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Project-led revenue with variable recurring components
caveats: No reopened source quantifies the recurring-revenue share for this NAICS code.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Meaningful report-and-analysis automation can produce staged uplift while validation, procurement and research-quality requirements keep the path to 50% effective adoption at roughly 2.5 years.

terminal_value.justification: AI and self-service threaten low-end study economics, but human primary data, defensible methods and trusted interpretation retain differentiated paid demand.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No: the supplied 4-digit 5419 occupation mix is dominated by veterinary services and is explicitly not a clean proxy for marketing research.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Competitive RFPs and fixed-price studies are the deflation channel; trackers, proprietary panels and high-consequence research are relatively stickier.

dataset_mismatch.description: The 4-digit 5419 deterministic occupation mix is dominated by veterinary occupations from sibling industries. It cannot represent 541910 labor composition, so the residual judgment carries the analytical thesis.
sensitivity: If actual analyst/operations labor is lower than the residual assumption, V falls.
If human fieldwork and senior moderation dominate, V falls despite strong report automation.

market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Commercial marketing research agencies and associated fieldwork (working hypothesis)
market_boundary.selection_basis: The NAICS definition covers marketing and public-opinion data collection and presentation; cited large data/platform transactions are adjacent evidence and are not treated as clean coverage of US 541910 boutiques.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: V is dominated by the acknowledged unusable occupation proxy and residual judgment.
M and underwriting returns depend on client concentration, recurring revenue and staff retention.

reviewer_note: Single-author initial fleet packet. Every cited score-driving URL was reopened on the stated access date. The packet does not represent code-specific ownership demographics or transaction terms as observed because the reopened sources did not provide them.

A.arithmetic: Python v3.1.2: A = 10 when t50=0 else min(10,10/t50); t50=2.5, A=4
